summary: The implementation has significant gaps in functionality. While the visual design (REQ-6) appears well-implemented with botanical colors and organic layouts, critical backend and frontend features are missing or incomplete. The backend lacks task execution logic, performance optimization, and a method to retrieve all tasks. The frontend cannot display actual task data because the backend doesn't provide a way to fetch task lists. Real-time updates, execution results, performance metrics, and task categorization logic are not implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement backend task processing system that parses and categorizes user instructions into executable task types
missing_requirements.reason: The backend createTask method accepts a TaskType parameter but does not parse or categorize instructions from natural language. No logic exists to analyze the description text and automatically determine task type, category, or parameters.

missing_requirements.requirement: Invalid or unclear instructions return appropriate error messages
missing_requirements.reason: No validation logic exists in createTask to check if instructions are valid, clear, or meaningful. The method accepts any text without validation or error handling for unclear instructions.

missing_requirements.requirement: Create task execution engine that processes categorized tasks and generates results
missing_requirements.reason: No execution engine exists. The backend stores tasks but contains no logic to actually execute them, process them based on category, or generate results. Task status remains pending with no execution mechanism.

missing_requirements.requirement: Results are generated and stored with execution metadata
missing_requirements.reason: While Task type has a result field, no code generates results or stores execution metadata (timestamps, execution duration, etc.). The result field remains null.

missing_requirements.requirement: Build task dashboard displaying submitted tasks, their status, and execution results in real-time
missing_requirements.reason: The dashboard cannot display actual tasks because useTasks returns an empty array. The backend lacks a getAllTasks or listTasks method, making it impossible to fetch and display user tasks.

missing_requirements.requirement: Interface updates automatically when task status changes
missing_requirements.reason: While polling is configured (refetchInterval: 3000ms), it cannot work because the tasks query returns an empty array. Without backend support for fetching tasks, real-time updates are non-functional.

missing_requirements.requirement: Performance metrics display execution speed for each task
missing_requirements.reason: No execution time tracking exists. The Task type lacks fields for execution start time, end time, or duration. Frontend displays attemptCount but no actual performance metrics.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement performance optimization system that prioritizes and executes tasks efficiently
missing_requirements.reason: No task prioritization or optimization system exists. While tasks have a priority field, there's no execution queue, scheduler, or logic to order tasks by dependencies or priority.

missing_requirements.requirement: System tracks and displays execution time for each task
missing_requirements.reason: No execution time tracking infrastructure exists. Task type lacks timestamp fields for execution start/end, and no timing logic is implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Performance benchmarks compare execution speed to typical human completion time
missing_requirements.reason: No benchmarking system exists. No human baseline times are stored, and no comparison logic is implemented to show AI vs human speed.

hallucinated_features.description: Task archiving system with archivedTasks map and HistoricalTask type
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest does not mention task archiving, historical task storage, or any archive functionality. This appears to be additional functionality not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Multiple utility functions for task attempt counting (hasAtLeastOneTaskAttempt, sumAttemptCounts, getAttemptCountsForTasks, isUnusedTask, getUnusedTaskCount)
hallucinated_features.reason: These specific utility functions for analyzing attempt counts across tasks were not requested. The requirement mentions tracking attempt counts but not these specific analysis functions.

hallucinated_features.description: Caffeine.ai attribution footer with UTM tracking parameters
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest does not mention caffeine.ai attribution or tracking. This appears to be a platform-specific addition not in the original requirements.
